validation.stdout_sample:
usage: skill_compute_gc_content.py [-h] --sequence SEQUENCE
                                   [--output {json,text}]

Compute the GC content of a DNA sequence.

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --sequence SEQUENCE   str value for sequence.
  --output {json,text}  Output format.

usage: skill_find_connected_components [-h] [--input INPUT]
                                       [--format {json,tsv,csv,space}]
                                       [--dedupe] [--include-isolated]
                                       [--nodes NODES] [--sort]
                                       [--output {json,text}]

Compute connected components in an undirected graph from an edge list.

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --input INPUT         Path to an input file. If omitted, read from stdin.
  --format {json,tsv,csv,space}
                        Input format.
  --dedupe              Remove duplicate undirected edges.
  --include-isolated    Include isolated nodes provided via --nodes as
                        singleton components.
  --nodes NODES         Comma-separated list of nodes (used with --include-
                        isolated).
  --sort                Sort nodes within components and sort components for
                        stable output.
  --output {json,text}  Output format.

usage: skill_reverse_complement.py [-h] [--sequence SEQUENCE]
                                   [--allow-ambiguous] [--strip-whitespace]
                                   [--output {json,text}]

Compute the reverse complement of a DNA sequence from an argument or stdin.

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --sequence SEQUENCE   Input DNA sequence. If omitted, read from stdin.
  --allow-ambiguous     Allow IUPAC ambiguous DNA codes (e.g., N, R, Y). If
                        set, ambiguous codes are complemented where defined;
                        otherwise input must contain only A/C/G/T (case-
                        insensitive).
  --strip-whitespace    Remove all whitespace characters from the input
                        sequence before processing.
  --output {json,text}  Output format: json or text.

usage: skill_summarize_numeric_columns.py [-h] [--input INPUT]
                                          [--include-counts]
                                          [--output {json,text}] [--pretty]

Compute min, max, and mean for numeric columns across a list of row
dictionaries. Reads JSON from a file or stdin.

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --input INPUT         Path to input JSON file containing rows (list of
                        objects). If omitted or '-', reads from stdin.
  --include-counts      Include per-column count of numeric values used in the
                        summary.
  --output {json,text}  Output format: json or text. Default is json.
  --pretty              Pretty-print JSON output with indentation (JSON output
                        only).
